note: Ground-truth multiple-choice questions verified against the source at tag 0.26.7. The 'answer' and 'source' fields are NEVER rendered into the agent prompt during act(); they are used only by score() and for human maintenance. Each answer has exactly one correct choice.

questions.question: In which module is the main `Typer` application class defined?
When a `Typer` instance is invoked (its `__call__` method runs), what does it do to actually execute the CLI?

choices.A: It builds a Click command via `get_command(self)` and calls it
choices.B: It directly parses `sys.argv` and dispatches to the registered functions
choices.C: It spawns a subprocess running the Click CLI
choices.D: It calls `argparse.ArgumentParser.parse_args()` on the registered commands

questions.question: Typer 0.26.7 vendors Click internally under `typer/_click/`. Which Click release is that vendored code adapted from?
questions.source: typer/_click/__init__.py docstring - 'Code taken and adapted from Click ... releases/tag/8.3.1'

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a runtime dependency declared in typer 0.26.7's pyproject.toml?
questions.source: pyproject.toml dependencies = [shellingham, rich, annotated-doc, colorama(Windows)]; click is vendored, not a dependency

questions.question: What is the purpose of the internal `Default()` function and the `DefaultPlaceholder` class?

choices.A: To cache parsed CLI defaults across invocations for performance
choices.B: To recognise when a default value has been explicitly overwritten, even if the new value is `None`
choices.C: To provide default values for missing environment variables
choices.D: To mark parameters as required when no default is supplied

questions.question: In which module are the `TyperCommand` and `TyperGroup` classes defined?
What does the top-level `typer.run(function)` helper do?

choices.A: Runs the function in a background thread and returns immediately
choices.B: Creates a `Typer(add_completion=False)` app, registers the function as a command, and invokes the app
choices.C: Registers the function as a callback on a global default Typer instance
choices.D: Validates the function's type hints and returns a generated CLI string

questions.source: typer/main.py:1891 run() - app = Typer(add_completion=False); app.command()(function); app()

questions.question: Which module provides `CliRunner`, the helper used to invoke a Typer CLI in tests in an isolated environment?
questions.source: typer/testing.py:128 - class CliRunner ('Based on functionality from Click')

questions.question: In which module are the public `Option` and `Argument` parameter factory functions defined?
What is the relationship between `OptionInfo`, `ArgumentInfo`, and `ParameterInfo` in `typer/models.py`?

choices.A: All three are unrelated and defined independently

questions.question: The `--install-completion` / `--show-completion` options are wired up through callbacks. In which module are `install_callback` and `show_callback` defined?
Which module is responsible for rendering Typer's Rich-formatted help output (panels, markup modes such as 'markdown' and 'rich')?
